frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Arre, aajkal ki ladki seedha mana kare toh uska kya bharosa, bas nakhra bhi hota hai. Pehle pooch lo kisi ke saath hai kya, pati-vati ya koi aadmi ho toh phir peeche hatna chahiye, warna aadmi ko koshish toh karni hi chahiye. Izzat us ghar ki bhi rakhni hoti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because the speaker treats a woman's own refusal as less valid than her attachment to another man. It encodes the surface manifestation of ignoring direct disinterest until confirming she is 'taken,' framed as respect for another man or household.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 52. Married. A farmer in rural Maharashtra. Your younger brother's wife has just given birth to their first child Ñ a son. There is celebration in the house. You are sitting with four male relatives outside after the celebration. One relative mentions that your own daughter in law has been married three years and there is no news yet. You are responding to this comment and sharing what you think about it and what you have been telling your son in private and what you believe a daughter in law's primary responsibility is in a marriage.
